# Data Model: Substantive Interaction Model

**Feature**: 016 Substantive Interaction Model  
**Branch**: `016-substantive-interaction-model`  
**Phase**: 1 — Design  
**Date**: 2026-05-13

---

## Overview

Feature 016 introduces no database schema. Its data model is the set of governed logical entities
that encode boundary authorization, handoff content, validator decisions, exemption metadata, and
corpus evolution for the console-first interaction model.

---

## Entity 1: Boundary Authorization Record

**Definition**: A `.squad/decisions.md` entry auto-generated by Squad from a user authorization
paste and reviewed/overridden during boundary inspection if needed.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `decision-id` | string | yes | Canonical pattern `authorization-feature-016-iter-<NNN>-<boundary-name>` or equivalent feature-local form |
| `type` | enum | yes | `authorization` or `sign-off` |
| `boundary` | enum | yes | One boundary only: planning, hardening-gate-signoff, implementation, review-boundary, review-verdict-signoff, retro-boundary, iteration-closeout, feature-closeout |
| `approving-human` | string | yes | Human approver name |
| `recorded-at` | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp | yes | Auto-generated by Squad |
| `commit-reference` | string | yes | `pending` or specific short hash |
| `authorization-text` | string | yes | Verbatim user authorization phrase |
| `source-paste-id` | string | no | Optional linkage when one pasted block yields paired entries |
| `override-notes` | string | no | Present only when the user edits generated metadata during inspection |

### Validation Rules

- Exactly one boundary per record
- `authorization-text` must preserve verbatim user wording
- `recorded-at` must use canonical UTC ISO 8601 **seconds precision** (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`) for
  newly generated Feature 016 entries
- Multi-boundary user paste for adjacent hardening-gate sign-off + implementation authorization
  produces **two records**, not one
- Validator scope inspects the generated record, not the raw user chat text
- `commit-reference` may begin as `pending`, but the post-commit verification cycle must synchronize
  it to the actual boundary commit hash before the boundary is considered verified

### State Transitions

```text
parsed-from-user-paste
  -> generated-pending-review
  -> confirmed
  -> commit-reference-synchronized
  -> overridden-and-confirmed
```

---

## Entity 2: Boundary Authorization Pair

**Definition**: A logical pairing of two adjacent authorization records created from one pasted user
authorization block covering hardening-gate sign-off and implementation authorization.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `source-paste-id` | string | yes | Shared correlation key across both generated entries |
| `entry-a-boundary` | enum | yes | `hardening-gate-signoff` |
| `entry-b-boundary` | enum | yes | `implementation` |
| `paired-record-count` | integer | yes | Must equal `2` |
| `shared-authorization-text` | string | yes | Same verbatim source phrase |

### Validation Rules

- Pairing is allowed only for the approved adjacent boundary combination
- Pairing does **not** authorize more than one boundary advance at runtime; it records two adjacent
  human decisions from one pasted block
- A single combined record is invalid

---

## Entity 3: Boundary Handoff Message

**Definition**: The Squad-authored three-section final stop message rendered at a lifecycle boundary.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `handoff-id` | string | no | Optional internal/log correlation |
| `boundary-name` | enum | yes | Specific lifecycle boundary being entered |
| `what-i-just-did` | rich text | yes | Substantive work summary |
| `why-i-stopped` | rich text | yes | Names the exact boundary and stop reason |
| `what-i-need-from-you` | rich text | yes | Actionable request naming boundary, inspection target, verdict |
| `artifact-links` | list of `file:///` URIs | no | Referenced inspection targets |
| `identifier-count` | integer | derived | Count of commits, `file:///` paths, FR refs, task IDs, decision refs |
| `word-count` | integer | derived | Word count for `what-i-just-did` |
| `authored-by-squad` | boolean | yes | Must be true for Feature 016 validator scope |

### Validation Rules

- Uses the three-section format for real human-blocked stops
- `what-i-just-did` thresholds:
  - planning / implementation / review / retro: `identifier-count >= 3` **and**
    `word-count >= 50`
  - iteration-closeout / feature-closeout: `identifier-count >= 3` **or**
    `word-count >= 50`
- `why-i-stopped` must name the exact boundary phase
- `what-i-need-from-you` must name:
  - boundary being authorized
  - inspection target(s) as `file:///` references where applicable
  - verdict required

### State Transitions

```text
drafted-by-coordinator
  -> validated-soft
  -> emitted-to-user
```

Soft warnings may attach during `validated-soft`; they do not block emission for Pillar 2.

---

## Entity 4: Boundary Commit Signature

**Definition**: A canonical commit-subject pattern used by the validator to classify lifecycle
boundaries mechanically.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `boundary-name` | enum | yes | Matches the lifecycle boundary set |
| `subject-regex` | regex string | yes | Canonical pattern from the approved spec |
| `iteration-scoped` | boolean | yes | True for the 7 per-iteration boundaries |
| `feature-level` | boolean | yes | True only for `feature-closeout` |

### Validation Rules

- Classification is subject-line-pattern based only
- Commit-body/content parsing is out of scope
- The validator uses commit signatures plus authorization records to detect bundled boundary advance

---

## Entity 5: Interaction-Model Validator Rule

**Definition**: A hard-fail or soft-warning rule enforcing one aspect of the three-pillar interaction
model.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `rule-id` | string | yes | Example: `validation-fail.bundled-boundary-advance` |
| `pillar` | enum | yes | `boundary-discipline`, `essence-in-console`, `click-through-navigation`, or `cross-cutting` |
| `severity` | enum | yes | `soft-warning` or `validation-fail` |
| `input-scope` | enum | yes | `squad-authored-handoffs`, `squad-authored-artifacts`, `decisions-ledger`, `commit-history` |
| `trigger-condition` | string | yes | Mechanical description of the finding |
| `output-shape` | string | yes | Warning or FAIL schema |
| `iteration-rollout` | string | yes | Iteration-specific behaviour if applicable |

### Planned Feature 016 Rule Set

| Rule ID | Severity in Feature 016 | Scope |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `validation-fail.bundled-boundary-advance` | hard fail | commit history + decisions ledger |
| `soft-warning.thin-what-i-just-did` | soft warning | Squad-authored boundary handoffs |
| `soft-warning.unspecific-stop-boundary` | soft warning | Squad-authored boundary handoffs |
| `soft-warning.unactionable-user-request` | soft warning | Squad-authored boundary handoffs |
| `soft-warning.bare-path-in-boundary-handoff` | Iteration 1 only | Squad-authored boundary handoffs |
| `validation-fail.bare-path-in-boundary-handoff` | Iteration 2 after proof | Squad-authored boundary handoffs |
| `soft-warning.bare-path-in-narration` | soft warning | Squad-authored non-boundary narration |
| `soft-warning.broken-file-url-reference` | soft warning | Squad-authored `file:///` references |

### Severity Transition Rule

The **detector** for `bare-path-in-boundary-handoff` remains constant. Only the configured severity
changes after Iteration 2 proof exists.

---

## Entity 6: Bare-Path Exemption Context

**Definition**: A context in which a path-like token is not treated as a click-through navigation
violation.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `context-id` | string | yes | Example: `inline-code`, `json-literal` |
| `matcher` | description/regex | yes | Detection rule for the exempt context |
| `source` | enum | yes | `built-in` or `project-extension` |
| `approval-required` | boolean | yes | Always true for project-extension |
| `approver-name` | string | conditional | Required when `source = project-extension` |
| `approval-rationale` | string | conditional | Required when `source = project-extension` |

### Built-In Exemptions

- fenced code blocks
- inline code spans
- shell-command arguments
- log output
- JSON literal values
- YAML literal values
- regex patterns
- file glob arguments
- existing URL contexts

### Validation Rules

- Project-specific exemptions live in `.specrew/config.yml`
- Each project-specific exemption requires recorded human approval, approver name, and rationale
- Exemption-list integration tests must prove bounded false positives before severity graduation

---

## Entity 7: Known Trap Row

**Definition**: A corpus entry describing a recurring interaction-model failure pattern, its
enforcement status, and proof references.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `category` | string | yes | `boundary-discipline` or `interaction-model` |
| `trap-id` | string | yes | Example: `bundled-boundary-advance` |
| `detection-method` | string | yes | Validator and/or replay proof |
| `remediation-guidance` | string | yes | Human-readable repair path |
| `linked-frs` | list | yes | Implementing FR references |
| `linked-tests` | list | no | Replay fixture references |
| `historical-cross-refs` | list | no | Prior corpus rows informing the evolution |
| `enforcement-status` | enum | yes | `validator-enforced`, `soft-warning-only`, `passive-candidate` |

### Planned Feature 016 Rows

- `bundled-boundary-advance`
- `thin-handoff-summary`
- `bare-path-in-handoff`
- `thin-artifact-content` (passive/future-candidate only)

---

## Entity 8: Post-Commit Verification Record

**Definition**: A planning-time logical record describing the exact checks that must complete after a
boundary commit before the handoff, ledger, and docs may claim the boundary is ready for the next
human decision.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `boundary-name` | enum | yes | Specific boundary just committed |
| `commit-hash` | string | yes | Actual commit hash or short hash under verification |
| `decisions-ledger-sync` | enum | yes | `pending`, `synchronized`, or `verified` |
| `timestamp-format-check` | enum | yes | Confirms seconds-precision canonical form |
| `stale-reference-scan` | enum | yes | `not-run`, `clean`, or `needs-fix` |
| `validator-rerun` | enum | yes | `not-run`, `pass`, or `fail` |
| `evidence-paths` | list | no | README/template/quickstart/review references used to prove the check |
| `notes` | string | no | Human-readable explanation for exceptions or deferred follow-up |

### Validation Rules

- Every post-commit verification record points at one boundary commit only
- `decisions-ledger-sync = synchronized` requires the corresponding authorization entry to use the
  real commit hash rather than `pending`
- `timestamp-format-check` enforces canonical seconds precision for newly authored entries
- `stale-reference-scan` must check the boundary handoff and cited artifacts after the final commit
- `validator-rerun` must refer to the exact committed tree, not a pre-commit working state

### State Transitions

```text
planned
  -> boundary-committed
  -> ledger-synchronized
  -> stale-reference-scanned
  -> validator-rerun
  -> verified
```

---

## Entity Relationships

```text
Boundary Authorization Record
  └── may participate in Boundary Authorization Pair

Boundary Authorization Record
  └── must reconcile through Post-Commit Verification Record before boundary readiness is claimed

Boundary Authorization Record + Boundary Commit Signature
  └── feed Interaction-Model Validator Rule
      └── detects bundled-boundary-advance

Boundary Handoff Message
  ├── contains file:/// artifact links
  ├── may trigger Interaction-Model Validator Rules
  └── may rely on Bare-Path Exemption Contexts

Interaction-Model Validator Rule
  └── maps to Known Trap Row
      └── may cross-reference historical corpus rows

Boundary Handoff Message
  └── feeds Post-Commit Verification Record
      └── checks stale references and exact-tree verification claims
```

---

## State Transition Summary

| Entity | Before Feature 016 | Iteration 1 Target | Iteration 2 Target |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Boundary authorization recording | Informal / inconsistent | Canonical single-boundary shape + paired-entry expansion | Same shape retained |
| Commit-reference synchronization | Manual / inconsistent | Pending placeholder allowed | Post-commit sync protocol made explicit |
| Ledger timestamp precision | Implicit | UTC ISO 8601 | Canonical UTC seconds precision documented and enforced in authored examples |
| Boundary handoff substance | Existing three sections, insufficiently enforced | Soft-warning enforcement for substantive sections | Same soft-warning-only posture |
| Bare-path in boundary handoff | Convention only | Soft warning with exemption support | Hard fail after bounded false-positive proof |
| Known-trap coverage | Prior handoff-related rows only | No new rows yet | New rows + historical cross-references |
| README/template lifecycle guidance | General lifecycle guidance | unchanged | Updated with three-pillar interaction model and post-commit verification protocol |
